title: Rosa dumalis f. innocua
what_description: Rosa dumalis f. innocua is a botanical variety of Rosa dumalis, a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is characterized by deciduous shrubs or small trees with thorny stems, 2-4 cm long, and dark green, serrate leaves, 5-10 cm long. The flowers are pink to white, 5-6 cm in diameter, with 5 petals and a cluster of stamens in the center. The fruit is a red, 1-2 cm long, aggregate pome, composed of 5-10 follicles. Rosa dumalis f. innocua is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rosa dumalis f. innocua are inflammation, fungal infections, wound healing, and skin irritation, due to its anti-inflammatory properties and flavonoids that inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria. Rosa dumalis f. innocua has also been shown to manage diabetes through its ability to stimulate glucose uptake in cells and reduce oxidative stress. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds accelerate wound healing, and its antiseptic properties help to prevent infections. Furthermore, it has been found to prevent cancer through its ability to inhibit the proliferation of cancer cells and induce apoptosis, and its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties also alleviate eczema and insect bites. Its antifungal and antioxidant properties also help to reduce fever and alleviate gastrointestinal issues, hypertension, pain relief, arthritis, digestive issues, and antiviral properties, as well as treat psoriasis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rosa dumalis f. innocua are antioxidant, which is attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids that scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are due to the presence of saponins and volatile oils that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, Rosa dumalis f. innocua exhibits antimicrobial activity against various pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, which is attributed to its bioactive compounds like terpenes and alkaloids that disrupt microbial cell membranes and inhibit their growth. Furthermore, the plant's analgesic properties are mediated by its alkaloids and volatile oils that interact with opioid receptors and reduce pain perception, providing relief from conditions such as headaches and muscle aches. Its cardioprotective effects are attributed to the flavonoids and phenolic acids that have vasodilatory and antioxidant effects, reducing blood pressure and preventing cardiovascular diseases.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rosa dumalis f. innocua are quercetin, a potent flavonoid antioxidant that inhibits inflammation and oxidative stress, rosmarinic acid, a diterpene that exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, gallic acid, a polyphenol that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, kaempferol, a flavonoid that inhibits cancer cell proliferation and induces apoptosis, and ellagic acid, a polyphenol that inhibits cancer cell growth and induces apoptosis by modulating cellular signaling pathways. Quercetin and kaempferol also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes. Rosmarinic acid and gallic acid exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms and scavenging free radicals. Ellagic acid also exhibits anti-cancer properties by inducing apoptosis and inhibiting cell cycle progression in cancer cells. These constituents work synergistically to provide a comprehensive range of health benefits.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rosa dumalis f. innocua are derived from its flowers and fruits. Rose petal tea is made by infusing dried rose petals in hot water, rich in antioxidants and flavonoids which may help reduce stress and inflammation. Rosewater, obtained through steam distillation, is a fragrant liquid used in skincare products, potentially soothing skin irritations and promoting wound healing. Rosehip oil, extracted from the fruit seeds, contains essential fatty acids and vitamins, claimed to improve skin hydration, reduce fine lines, and support tissue repair. Additionally, rose petal jam, rose syrup, and other products are also produced from the flowers, often used as culinary and medicinal remedies for digestive issues and as a natural preservative.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Rosa dumalis f. innocua improperly include experiencing allergic reactions, which can manifest as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing due to the plant's potential to produce allergenic compounds. Additionally, improper use may lead to skin irritation, characterized by redness, swelling, and inflammation, as the plant's sap can cause phototoxicity and contact dermatitis. Furthermore, Rosa dumalis f. innocua may exacerbate existing allergies, particularly those related to the Asteraceae family, by releasing allergenic particles into the air when dried or crushed. Ingestion of the plant can also induce vomiting due to the presence of toxic compounds like phenolic glycosides and flavonoids, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation. Improper handling and use of Rosa dumalis f. innocua can also lead to other systemic effects, including renal and hepatic toxicity, due to the plant's potential to release nephrotoxic and hepatotoxic metabolites.
